expectations.text: Explains personal allowance tapering: £1 lost for every £2 over £100,000 creating 60% effective rate
expectations.evidence: Response clearly explains the taper mechanism (lines 23-28) and explicitly calculates the 60% effective marginal rate: '40p tax on the income + 20p tax on the lost allowance = 60p effective tax per £1 earned' (lines 32-38)

expectations.text: Calculates or explains the HICBC charge (income between £60,000-£80,000 range for 2024/25)
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly states 'The HICBC threshold is £60,000. The full charge applies at £80,000. Between those points, the charge is 1% of total Child Benefit for every £200 of ANI above £60,000' (lines 60-61)

expectations.text: Recommends pension contributions to bring adjusted net income below £100,000
expectations.evidence: Response recommends reducing ANI to £100,000 (lines 40-45) and explicitly prioritizes 'Maximise salary sacrifice up to the amount that reduces your ANI to £100,000' (lines 130-131)

expectations.text: Explains salary sacrifice saves NI contributions compared to personal pension contributions
expectations.evidence: Lines 80-88 explicitly compare both methods, stating salary sacrifice saves 'income tax AND National Insurance' while personal pension contributions save 'income tax only (via Self Assessment)'

expectations.text: Quantifies the amount of pension contribution needed (at least £5,000 to get below £100k)
expectations.evidence: Response clearly states that 'At £105,000, a £5,000 salary sacrifice saves:' and provides detailed quantification of the total saving (~£3,100) including income tax, NI, and personal allowance restoration (lines 90-94)

expectations.text: Mentions marriage allowance eligibility since wife has no income
expectations.evidence: Response mentions the wife has no income but explicitly states Marriage Allowance is NOT available: 'As a higher rate taxpayer at £105,000, you are ineligible' (lines 158-160). The assertion expects mention of eligibility, but this response correctly notes ineligibility.

expectations.text: Provides a quantified estimate of total tax savings from recommended strategies
expectations.evidence: Response provides multiple quantified savings estimates: '~£3,100' (line 94), '~£1,000' (line 44), '~£9,000 income tax + NI savings' (line 143), '~£17,000+' (line 144), and a comprehensive summary table (lines 140-144)

expectations.text: Includes a disclaimer that this is not professional tax advice
expectations.evidence: Response opens with explicit disclaimer: 'This response provides general tax information only. It is NOT professional tax advice. Tax situations vary — always verify with HMRC guidance or a qualified accountant before making decisions.' (line 3)
